sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Bournemouth, currently sitting at 9th in the league with 53 points, host Aston Villa, who are in 7th place with 60 points, at the Vitality Stadium in Bournemouth. This matchup promises to be intriguing as both teams are in the mid-to-upper tier of the table. The referee, Stuart Attwell, averages 3.4 yellow cards per game and 0.13 red cards, suggesting a moderately disciplined encounter could be expected.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last meeting on October 26, 2024, Aston Villa and Bournemouth played out a 1-1 draw, with Bournemouth’s Mark Travers earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.6 Sofascore rating. Over the last 5 encounters between these sides, there have been 1 win for Bournemouth, 2 draws, and 2 wins for Aston Villa. Head-to-head trends show no losses for Aston Villa in the last 4 games against Bournemouth, while both teams have scored in 6 of their last 8 meetings. Additionally, 6 of the last 8 games have seen over 2.5 goals, and 5 of the last 6 matches had more than 4.5 cards.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Bournemouth come into this match with a solid streak of no losses in their last 5 games across all competitions. They’ve also seen both teams scoring in 8 of their last 10 matches. Aston Villa, on the other hand, have been involved in high-scoring games, with over 2.5 goals in 9 of their last 10 away matches. Notably, Aston Villa have not kept a clean sheet in their last 3 head-to-head games, while Bournemouth have struggled defensively too, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 meetings with Villa.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Bournemouth
sections.text: Bournemouth will look to Justin Kluivert, a midfielder with 12 goals and 6 assists this season, who has overperformed his expected goals (xG) by 2.222. Evanilson, a forward with 10 goals, has been in form recently, scoring 3 goals in his last 5 matches. Antoine Semenyo, another key midfielder, has contributed 9 goals and 5 assists, while defender Dean Huijsen has a high average rating of 7.25. Also, keep an eye on Marcos Senesi, who ranks 4th in the league for the percentage of long balls in total passes at 10.7%.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Aston Villa
sections.text: Aston Villa’s attack will be spearheaded by Ollie Watkins, a forward with 15 goals and 7 assists, closely matching his xG of 14.79. Watkins has also been a standout in past meetings with Bournemouth, averaging a 7.38 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matchups with 1 goal and 4 assists. Morgan Rogers, a midfielder with 8 goals and 8 assists, is another threat. Defensively, Tyrone Mings ranks 1st in the league for ground duels won at 88.9%, while Ezri Konsa is 3rd at 75.9%. John McGinn, often fouled 2.2 times per game (3rd in the league), will be pivotal in midfield.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics - Bournemouth
sections.text: Bournemouth have scored 55 goals this season, ranking 9th in the league, and overperformed their expected goals by 6.3977, ranking 3rd for xG overperformance. Defensively, they’ve conceded 42 goals, which places them 4th for least goals conceded. They excel in pressing, ranking 1st for possessions won in the final third per game at 5.857, and 3rd for penalty area entries per game at 31.057. Their corners per game stand at 6.028, ranking 7th, while they’ve hit the woodwork an unlucky 22 times, the most in the league.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics - Aston Villa
sections.text: Aston Villa’s goal distribution shows they are particularly dangerous late in games, with 28.57% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Their defensive solidity is evident with players like Mings and Konsa ranking high in ground duels won. However, they’ve been missing key midfield creativity with Youri Tielemans out, who ranks 5th in the league for big chances created with 16. Villa will need to capitalize on Bournemouth’s vulnerability, as the home side ranks 1st for fouls committed with 474 this season.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Bournemouth’s strength lies in their pressing game, ranking 1st for interceptions per game at 9.571 and possessions won in the final third. They also tend to score heavily between the 61st and 75th minutes, with 21.43% of their goals in this period. Aston Villa, conversely, will likely exploit set-pieces and late opportunities, given their goal distribution. Head-to-head stats suggest Bournemouth often score first in 6 of their last 8 meetings, so Villa’s defense must be alert early on.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Bournemouth will miss forward Dango Ouattara, who has 7 goals and 4 assists, and midfielder Ryan Christie with 2 goals and 3 assists, potentially affecting their attacking depth. Aston Villa are without midfielder Youri Tielemans, a significant loss given his 7 assists and league-leading creativity. Marcus Rashford’s absence, with 6 goals and 3 assists, further dents Villa’s attacking options, placing more responsibility on Watkins and Rogers to deliver.
